setting.note: Jagua Nana, an ageing but still magnetic Lagos courtesan nicknamed for her Jaguar-car glamour, moves through the nightclubs, markets and thug-politics of the booming city as she clings to youth and to her young lover Freddie. Ekwensi maps the real Lagos: the nightclubs of the island where Jagua reigns, the colonial Marina, the crowded slums and markets of old Lagos, and the political rallies where money and muscle decide everything.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Cyprian Ekwensi, first published 1961. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Jagua Nana, Cyprian Ekwensi (1961). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The gold glamour of Jagua herself, the expensive style and fading beauty she stakes everything on. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
